articles.title: Inflation’s new shock: the Iran war, gasoline and the Fed’s narrowing path
articles.summary: March’s price spike has shifted the economics debate from disinflation to damage control, with gasoline, tariffs and consumer psychology now pressing on the same fragile point.

articles.title: Tiny NASA Spacecraft’s First Images Point to a New Era of Exoplanet Science
articles.summary: SPARCS is only a CubeSat, but its first ultraviolet images suggest a specialized approach to measuring stellar radiation could sharpen the search for habitable worlds.

articles.title: OpenAI’s latest moves show the AI race shifting from models to operations, safety and trust
articles.summary: In a run of announcements this spring, OpenAI has pushed beyond model launches into safety research, abuse reporting, and a more hands-on role in the public AI conversation—an indicator that the industry’s next battleground may be operational rigor rather than raw benchmark gains.

articles.title: Earth at Night Is Brightening, But Not Everywhere—and That Matters
articles.summary: A new Nature study, paired with Reuters reporting and NASA imagery, shows the planet’s night lights are rising in complex, uneven ways—revealing how energy policy, urban growth and light pollution are reshaping the after-dark world.

articles.title: U.S.-Iran Talks Collapse in Islamabad as Strait of Hormuz Tensions Push the Gulf Toward a Wider Crisis
articles.summary: After 21 hours of negotiations in Pakistan, the United States and Iran left without a deal, leaving a fragile ceasefire uncertain and putting renewed pressure on the world’s most strategically sensitive waterway.

articles.title: Iran Talks Collapse in Islamabad, Deepening a War That Still Threatens the World Economy
articles.summary: A weekend of direct negotiations in Pakistan ended without agreement, leaving the fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran in doubt and renewing fears of fresh fighting, shipping disruption and another shock to energy markets.

articles.title: Trump’s bid to erase birthright citizenship turns the Supreme Court into the front line of a larger constitutional fight
articles.summary: After a consequential Supreme Court hearing and a wave of lower-court setbacks, the White House’s push to narrow who is entitled to citizenship at birth now sits at the center of a battle over immigration, executive power and the meaning of the Fourteenth Amendment.

articles.title: NASA’s Artemis II photos turn the Moon into a live science laboratory
articles.summary: The crew’s April 6 lunar-flyby images are more than a postcard from deep space: they are fresh data on lighting, optics, human perception, and how astronauts will work around the Moon.

articles.title: OpenAI’s security scare and enterprise push show how AI’s next battle is trust, not just intelligence
articles.summary: A week of OpenAI disclosures — from a developer-tool compromise to new enterprise and policy bets — underscores how the AI market is shifting from model hype to operational trust, governance and distribution.

articles.title: Meta’s new AI push shows the industry’s next fight is not just model quality, but where intelligence lives
articles.summary: A new Meta model, tighter product integration, and expanding infrastructure bets point to a broader shift in 2026: the companies building AI are racing to control the assistant layer, the data center layer, and the consumer relationship at once.
